paragraph: Single-family homes in Elmwood Park are moving in a real seller's market. Forty sales over seven months at a median of $630,000 tell you that priced-right homes are getting multiple offers. Fifty-seven percent of homes sold above asking, the median days on market sits at nineteen and a half, and the sale-to-list ratio is running at 1.01. Sellers are dictating terms here, and you'll see homes hit contract quickly if they're positioned correctly. Multi-family properties, by contrast, are even hotter. Nineteen sales at a median of $865,000 show strong investor and owner-occupant demand, with eighty-four percent selling above list and a sale-to-list ratio of 1.06. These homes are moving in eighteen to nineteen days on average. Condo and townhouse inventory is sparse in this market, with only four sales at a median of $454,500, though the ratio and days on market suggest balanced pricing when units do come available. If you're looking in Elmwood Park, the single-family market is where most activity is, and you'll need a competitive offer to win. Multi-family properties are moving faster and commanding stronger appreciation over asking.
